source.title: Virological characteristics of the SARS-CoV-2 XBB variant derived from recombination of two Omicron subvariants

initial_request.research_problem: Investigate the virological characteristics of the SARS-CoV-2 XBB variant, a recombinant lineage from two Omicron subvariants (BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1), to understand how recombination impacts viral fitness, immune evasion, receptor binding, fusogenicity, and pathogenicity, and to explain XBB's rapid global spread.
initial_request.known_context: XBB emerged through recombination of BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1, with a breakpoint in the RBD. It shows higher effective reproduction number (Re) than parental lineages, indicating increased fitness through recombination.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: The study aims to investigate the virological characteristics of the XBB variant, which emerged through recombination of two Omicron subvariants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: The origin and fitness advantage of XBB are unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB emerged through recombination of BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1 and has higher fitness than parental lineages.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform phylogenetic and recombination analyses to determine origin and estimate Re.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB emerged through recombination of BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1 with breakpoint at position 22,920; tMRCA July 7, 2022.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Establishing recombination origin motivates investigation of fitness advantage.

evidence.quote_or_span: Our phylogenetic analyses suggested that XBB emerged through the recombination of two cocirculating BA.2 lineages, BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1 (a progeny of BA.2.75), during the summer of 2022.

Our analysis suggests that the XBB clade's most recent common ancestor (tMRCA) existed at the start of July 2022 (median posterior date: July 7, 2022; 95% HPD confidence intervals: from June 10, 2022, to July 29, 2022).
we first split the alignment used for the recombination analysis above at genome position 22,920 (the breakpoint inferred by RDP5)

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB emerged through recombination of BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether recombination directly increases fitness is unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB shows higher effective reproduction number (Re) than parental lineages, indicating increased fitness through recombination.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Estimate Re for XBB and parental lineages.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB shows substantially higher Re than parental lineages, suggesting recombination increased viral fitness.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Higher Re validates the novel finding of fitness increase via recombination.

evidence.quote_or_span: XBB shows substantially higher $R_{e}$ than the parental lineages, suggesting that the recombination event increased $R_{e}$ (i.e., viral fitness).
Our phylogenetic analyses suggested that XBB emerged through the recombination of two co-circulating BA.2 lineages, BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1 (a progeny of BA.2.75), during the summer of 2022

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB shows higher Re than parental lineages.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether recombination directly increases fitness is novel.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB is the first SARS-CoV-2 variant to increase fitness through recombination.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Validate the novelty of recombination-driven fitness increase.
evolution_trajectory.observation: This is the first documented example of a SARS-CoV-2 variant increasing its fitness through recombination rather than substitutions.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Novel fitness mechanism prompts investigation of immune resistance.

evidence.quote_or_span: To our knowledge, this is the first documented example of a SARS-CoV-2 variant increasing its fitness through recombination rather than substitutions.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB.1 is a subvariant of XBB.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Immune resistance of XBB.1 to breakthrough infection sera is unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB.1 exhibits profound resistance to BA.2 and BA.5 breakthrough infection sera.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform neutralization assays using pseudoviruses and sera from BA.2 and BA.5 breakthrough infections.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB.1 shows 30-fold resistance to BA.2 sera (P=0.0002) and 13-fold resistance to BA.5 sera (P<0.0001). 10/14 BA.2 sera and 9/20 BA.5 sera fail to neutralize.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Profound resistance leads to dissection of individual substitution contributions.

evidence.quote_or_span: we found that XBB.1 exhibits profound (30-fold) resistance to breakthrough BA.2 infection sera (P=0.0002, Fig. 2a).
XBB.1 exhibited profound (13-fold) resistance to breakthrough BA.5 infection sera (P<0.0001, Fig. 2b).
10 out of 14 breakthrough BA.2 infection sera and 9 out of 20 breakthrough BA.5 infection sera fail to neutralize XBB.1.
XBB.1 significantly escaped from monovalent vaccine sera (23.3-fold, P<0.0001), BA.1 bivalent vaccine sera (19-fold, P<0.0001), and BA.5 bivalent vaccine sera (41-fold, P<0.0001) compared with B.1.1.
1 sequences harbor XBB-characteristic substitutions (S:V83A, S:F486S, and S:F490S) probably due to the misclassification of XBB, we removed the sequences of BA.
5 sequences harboring any of the convergent S substitutions, S:R346X, S:K444X, and S:N460X from our dataset in order to exclude the sequences belonging to the recent BA.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which spike substitutions contribute to immune resistance?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Multiple single substitutions in XBB.1 S protein confer significant resistance to BA.2 and BA.5 sera, with cooperative effects.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test individual and combined substitutions using mutant pseudoviruses.
evolution_trajectory.observation: V83A (2.1-fold), Y144del (2.9-fold), F486S (3.0-fold), F490S (2.7-fold) confer resistance to BA.2 sera. Y144del and G446S cooperatively contribute to BA.5 resistance.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Understanding substitution effects informs vaccine escape assessment.

evidence.quote_or_span: several substitutions, such as V83A (2.1-fold, P=0.0034), Y144del (2.9-fold, P=0.0002), Q183E (2.0-fold, P=0.0039), R346T (2.1-fold, P=0.0005), L368I (1.8-fold, P=0.042), V445P (2.1-fold, P=0.0002), F486S (3.0-fold, P=0.0002), and F490S (2.7-fold, P=0.024), conferred significant resistance to breakthrough BA.2 infection sera.
our data suggest that multiple substitutions in the XBB.1S cooperatively contribute to the resistance against humoral immunity induced by breakthrough BA.2 infection.
these two mutations (Y144del and G446S) cooperatively contribute to the resistance against humoral immunity induced by breakthrough BA.5 infection.
XBB.1 significantly escaped from monovalent vaccine sera (23.3-fold, P<0.0001), BA.1 bivalent vaccine sera (19-fold, P<0.0001), and BA.5 bivalent vaccine sera (41-fold, P<0.0001) compared with B.1.1.
1 sequences harbor XBB-characteristic substitutions (S:V83A, S:F486S, and S:F490S) probably due to the misclassification of XBB, we removed the sequences of BA.
SARS-CoV-2 spike L452R variant evades cellular immunity and increases infectivity.

evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB.1 significantly escapes from 4-dose vaccine sera, including monovalent, BA.1 bivalent, and BA.5 bivalent vaccines.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test neutralization by sera from individuals receiving 4 doses of various vaccines.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB.1 escapes monovalent (23.3-fold), BA.1 bivalent (19-fold), and BA.5 bivalent (41-fold) vaccine sera (P<0.0001).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Vaccine escape data contributes to antigenic distinctness analysis.

evidence.quote_or_span: XBB.1 significantly escaped from monovalent vaccine sera (23.3-fold, P<0.0001), BA.1 bivalent vaccine sera (19-fold, P<0.0001), and BA.5 bivalent vaccine sera (41-fold, P<0.0001) compared with B.1.1.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How does XBB.1 RBD bind ACE2 compared to BA.2?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB.1 RBD has higher ACE2 binding affinity than BA.2, with F486S reducing affinity but compensated by other substitutions.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure ACE2 binding affinity of XBB.1 RBD and mutants using surface plasmon resonance.
evolution_trajectory.observation: R346T and L368I increase ACE2 binding affinity; F486S decreases affinity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: ACE2 binding affinity data leads to infectivity assessment.

evidence.quote_or_span: the R346T substitution, which is common in both XBB.1 and BQ.1.1, significantly increased the binding affinity of BA.2S RBD to human ACE2
the L368I substitution augmented ACE2 binding affinity
the F486S substitution significantly decreased ACE2 binding affinity

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB.1 RBD binds ACE2 with higher affinity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does higher ACE2 binding translate to increased infectivity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB.1 pseudovirus shows greater infectivity than BA.2, with R346T, L368I, and V83A increasing infectivity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure pseudovirus infectivity in HOS-ACE2/TMPRSS2 cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB.1 pseudovirus infectivity is 7.6-fold greater than BA.2. R346T (1.9-fold), L368I (2.2-fold), V83A (3.3-fold) increase infectivity; Y144del (0.18-fold) and G252V (0.54-fold) decrease it.

evidence.quote_or_span: the infectivity of the XBB.1 pseudovirus was 7.6-fold greater than that of the BA.2 pseudovirus
two substitutions in the RBD, R346T (1.9-fold) and L368I (2.2-fold), significantly increased pseudovirus infectivity
a substitution in the NTD, V83A (3.3-fold), significantly increased pseudovirus infectivity
two substitutions in the NTD, Y144del (0.18-fold) and G252V (0.54-fold), significantly decreased pseudovirus infectivity

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Is XBB.1 S more fusogenic than BA.2 and BA.2.75?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB.1 S is more fusogenic than BA.2 and BA.2.75 despite lower surface expression, with V83A, R346T, and N460K contributing.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform cell-based fusion assay with S proteins.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB.1 S is 2.2-fold more fusogenic than BA.2 S and 1.5-fold more than BA.2.75 S. V83A and R346T significantly increase fusogenicity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Fusogenicity results prompt structural analysis to explain mechanisms.

evidence.quote_or_span: XBB.1 S is significantly more fusogenic than BA.2 S (2.2-fold) and BA.2.75 S (1.5-fold)
two substitutions, V83A and R346T, significantly increased fusogenicity
two substitutions in the RBD (R346T and N460K) and a substitution in the NTD (V83A) contribute to the augmented fusogenicity of XBB.1 S

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB.1 S shows altered binding and fusogenicity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What are the structural bases for altered binding and immune evasion?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Cryo-EM structures show XBB.1 S predominantly in closed states, with F486S losing hydrophobic interactions with ACE2 and F490S altering interaction with ACE2 K31.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Determine cryo-EM structures of XBB.1 S ectodomain and S-ACE2 complex.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB.1 S ectodomain reconstructed in two closed states at 2.50 Å and 2.51 Å. S-ACE2 complex at 3.29 Å. F486S loses hydrophobic interactions with ACE2.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Structural insights inform in vitro replication and barrier disruption experiments.

evidence.quote_or_span: The XBB.1 S ectodomain was reconstructed as two closed states (closed-1 and closed-2) at resolutions of 2.50 Å and 2.51 Å
The XBB.1 S-ACE2 complex structures were reconstructed by cryo-EM analysis in both RBD one-up and two-up conformations
local refinement was performed on RBD-ACE2 and reconstructed at a resolution of 3.29 Å
the F486S substitution changed the side chain to a less bulky hydrophilic residue and lost hydrophobic interactions with the hydrophobic patch consisting of F28, L79, M82, and Y83 in ACE2
1 sequences harbor XBB-characteristic substitutions (S:V83A, S:F486S, and S:F490S) probably due to the misclassification of XBB, we removed the sequences of BA.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How does XBB.1 replicate in vitro and affect epithelial barriers?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: XBB.1 growth kinetics are comparable to BA.2.75 in most cell lines but lower in iPSC-derived alveolar epithelial cells; XBB.1 disrupts airway epithelial-endothelial barriers more than BA.2.75.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform growth kinetics in various cell lines and airway-on-a-chip barrier disruption assay.
evolution_trajectory.observation: XBB.1 less replicative than BA.2.75 in iPSC-derived alveolar epithelial cells. XBB.1 invasion into bottom channel of airway-on-chips significantly higher than BA.2.75.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: In vitro results lead to in vivo pathogenicity and transmission studies.

evidence.quote_or_span: XBB.1 was less replicative than BA.2.75 in this culture system (Fig. 5f).
the percentage of virus that invaded the bottom channel of XBB.1-infected airway-on-chips was significantly higher than that of BA.2.75-infected airway-on-chips (Fig. 5h).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: XBB.1 shows higher fusogenicity but lower pathogenicity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Why is there a discrepancy between fusogenicity and intrinsic pathogenicity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Discrepancy may be due to non-S mutations, theoretical limits, or hamster model differences.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Intrinsic pathogenicity of XBB.1 comparable or lower than BA.2.75. Non-S mutations may attenuate pathogenicity. Hamster model may not reproduce human condition.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Limitations highlight need for further studies.

evidence.quote_or_span: the intrinsic pathogenicity of XBB.1 was comparable or even lower than that of BA.2.75
certain mutations in the non-S region of the XBB.1 genome can attenuate viral pathogenicity augmented by the higher fusogenicity
a hamster model may not reproduce the human condition

it might be possible that the tropism and affinity of S proteins of these variants are different between human ACE2 and hamster ACE2, and therefore, a hamster model may not reproduce the human condition
1 sequences harbor XBB-characteristic substitutions (S:V83A, S:F486S, and S:F490S) probably due to the misclassification of XBB, we removed the sequences of BA.

validation_methods: In vitro growth kinetics and airway-on-a-chip
success_verification.final_conclusion: XBB variant emerged through recombination of BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1, increasing fitness via recombination. XBB.1 exhibits profound immune resistance, enhanced ACE2 binding, increased infectivity and fusogenicity, but lower intrinsic pathogenicity in hamsters compared to Delta, comparable to BA.2.75. Discrepancy between fusogenicity and pathogenicity may be due to non-S mutations or model limitations.
limitations: Discrepancy between fusogenicity and intrinsic pathogenicity may be due to non-S mutations, theoretical limits, or hamster model differences.
Hamster model may not reproduce human condition due to potential differences in ACE2 tropism and affinity.
